era_context: The Old Kingdom of Egypt (c. 2686–2181 BCE) continues under the Fourth Dynasty. Mesopotamia remains fragmented among city-states. The Indus Valley Civilisation is in its Early Harappan phase. Bronze Age societies dominate the Eastern Mediterranean.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of Asia outside the Indus Valley

events.title: Continuation of Fourth Dynasty Egypt under Khafre or Khufu
events.description: The Fourth Dynasty of Egypt continues its reign. Depending on the chronological system applied, this year falls within the reign of either Khufu (builder of the Great Pyramid) or Khafre (builder of the second pyramid and the Great Sphinx). The Old Kingdom remains at the height of its centralised power, with the state capable of mobilising vast resources for monumental construction. The exact ruler and specific events for this year are not documented in surviving sources.
key_figures: Khufu or Khafre – Pharaoh (depending on chronological system)
events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for this year. The assignment of this year to a particular reign depends on the chronological system used, which is itself contested.
cross_references: Part of Fourth Dynasty Egypt (c. 2613–2494 BCE conventional dating)

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for 2742 BCE. The assignment of this year to a particular pharaoh depends on contested chronological systems. No disconfirming evidence is identified because no specific claims are made about this year.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 2742 BCE is essentially non-existent. No specific events, inscriptions, or archaeological evidence can be assigned to this year with confidence. The year falls within the Fourth Dynasty, but the exact ruler and events are unknown. This reflects the general sparsity of year-by-year documentation for the Old Kingdom.
